headline: Nvidia Stock Keeps Heading Lower. Is It Time to Buy?
summary: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) shares fell on Monday as the artificial intelligence (AI) chip leader continues to show sensitivity to the broader market pullback.  Semiconductor stocks like Nvidia are known for being cyclical, so it's understandable that the stock would fall on the general stock market concerns, which include weakening consumer sentiment, stubborn inflation, intensifying tariffs, and other issues.  The news today that seemed to drive the market down was President Trump's reaffirming of "Liberation Day" or a new round of reciprocal tariffs that are set to go into effect on Wednesday.

headline: Citing AI Lag, Jefferies Cuts AMD Rating to Hold, Lowers Price Target to $120
summary: Jefferies downgraded AMD stock from Buy to Hold and lowered its price target from $135 to $120.

headline: BofA Says Nvidia Volatility May Persist Ahead of U.S. Export Rule Implementation
summary: Nvidia (NVDA, Financials) shares may remain volatile until mid-May as investors await clarity on the U.S. government's planned export controls on artificial intelligence semiconductors, according to Bank of America.  In a recent report, Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya said the volatility may last until May 15, when the United States plans to apply the "AI Diffusion Rule" of the Biden administration.  Arya called the day "Liberation Day," implying it may be a turning point in how Nvidia's China-related concerns are seen by markets.Through its H20 graphics processing chips, or GPUs, Arya said Nvidia had around 10% direct income exposure to China, which the suggested legislation might influence.

headline: Advanced Micro Devices, Robinhood, 23andMe: Market Minute
summary: Wealth host Brad Smith goes over some of the trading day's top stories on this segment of Market Minute. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock is under pressure after the company was downgraded by Jefferies, citing AMD's difficulty keeping up with Nvidia (NVDA). Robinhood (HOOD) introduces new banking services and wealth management services for customers. 23andMe (ME) skyrockets after receiving approval to sell customer data in its bankruptcy proceedings. Stay up to date on the latest market action, minute-by-minute, with Yahoo Finance's Market Minute.
